name: Lake Camping
description: If you want a lovely secluded camping spot continue on this forest road (side rd 3) to the small lake. When you get to the lake make a right along the grassy double track and you'll come to a fire pit by an old abandoned cabin.

name: Port-a-potty style toilets. No running water.
Washrooms at parking lot for park
You can take this trail for a short out-and-back to pretty lake
Tenting spot
description: For a secluded tenting/hammock area when you exit the park at the blue gate look for a trail across the road on left hand side. Cross the wooden bridge and after walking alongside the creek for about 150 meters you'll see a little opening in the trees on the right hand side. In among the pine trees is a large open area that would be great for tents and there is even a picnic table. Stream water can be treated.

name: Spring Water
description: Pipe with spring water flowing through it. About halfway up the short steep climb on the right. Perhaps best to treat it.

name: Water Tap
description: There is a outside water tap on the community centre but says not potable so would need to be treated.

name: Tenting Option
description: Flat area by creek is a good area to pitch a tent. Riders recommend this spot

name: Entering Mono Provincial Park.
description: Except for one tricky downhill section the trails in the park are fast, flowy and so much fun.

name: Rocky Uphill
description: Steep and rocky. A free butter tart for riders who make it clean.

name: Wild Camping
description: Flat spots after small bridge could be a nice place to rest for a night.

name: Wild Camping
description: Lots of potential tent sites along Lower Valley Road.

name: Wild Camping
description: Flat area by old chairlifts on Sideroad 7A is a great tenting spot.  Space for multiple tents. Plus, fantastic view. But don't go down to Kimberly first as it's a very tough climb back up to this camping spot.

name: Route Alternative
description: If needed an easier bypass would be to turn right here on sideroad 25 to 7th line. Take 7th south to meet up with the route.

name: Route Alternative
description: This section features a rough doubletrack. If needed, a detour would be to continue on 3rd Line to meet up with Old Mail Rd.

name: Tim Hortons
description: 5am to 9pm

name: Wild Camping
description: Probably can find a little spot down this trail to pitch a tent or string up a hammock.

name: Water Refill
description: Can fill up water bottles at Eagle Adventures. There is also 24 hour water access: The outside tap is inside the  doors for the stairs to the lower level. SE corner of Lodge. Or you can drink from the hose found on the right side of lodge.

name: Aid Station
description: Linda and Craig can provide assistance to riders as needed such as water, bike repair tools, hose for washing bike etc.  House located besides Mono Cliffs Inn.

name: Water Refill
description: Riders can obtain drinking water at cabin with 677225 address sign. Cabin (black with white trim) is located on the left about halfway up the steep climb. Just go around  closed gate (wooden gate has openings) and the tap with a shower attached is on left (west) side of the cabin.

name: Detour
description: Keep riding straight if you need supplies at the Hockley Valley store.

name: Private Camping
description:
Landowner at marker 677225 has offered BT 700 riders the option of camping on his cabin property for an evening. Beautiful area. Water from tap on left side of cabin is drinkable and can be used for showering.  Electrical outlets on cabin also work. 
Located half way up the big climb on the east side just after you cross a creek if you are traveling south. 

Reach the cabin owner at johndondertman@gmail.com or text/call him at 416 6059548 for permission and the lock combo.

name: Route Option
description: For a side-trip to two epic lookouts continue straight on this dirt road. When you get to a T-junction where the Bruce trail runs in about 1.5km make a right on the dirt road and you'll come to the lookouts. At the second lookout you need to backtrack as the road does not continue through. The first lookout is about 2km and the second is about 2.5km from this intersection.

name: Brant Tract Trails
description: Fun set of flowy trails and not technical. Sometimes trails are marked as closed because of rain but this route is the least muddy way through.  You can spend more time in here riding on additional trails. Very well market. Most are doable on a cross bike. The loop I recommend is Brainbuster, Rabbit Run, Red Pine Loop, Razor’s Run, The Shocker, The Maples, Devil’s Elbow, The Express and then back up Rabbit Run.  Above is the gps route for this.
